audit.aligned: - --bare mode used correctly in bootstrap/claude-config/commands/quick.md: "claude --bare -p $ARGUMENTS" with disable-model-invocation: true (no context loaded). - quick.md documents ANTHROPIC_API_KEY requirement for --bare mode (OAuth/keychain disabled). - quick.md documents combining --bare with --output-format json for machine-parseable output. - quick.md documents combining --bare with --model sonnet for cheaper queries. - GitHub Actions (claude-action.yml, claude-code-review.yml) use anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 which runs headless (-p) mode internally. - claude-code-review.yml passes claude_args with --max-turns 15 --max-budget-usd 5 (correct headless budget/turn limiting pattern). - claude-action.yml uses claude_code_oauth_token for authentication (correct for headless; not ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). - check-integrations.sh validates claude auth is active (required for OAuth flows). - quick.md documents combining --output-format json with --allowedTools. - bootstrap/phases/02-claude.sh references CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB which is a headless security best practice (prevents subprocess credential leakage).
audit.gaps:
1. --output-format json with --json-schema is not used anywhere in CI workflows or scripts. The docs show structured output via --json-schema with a JSON Schema definition which enables typed/validated JSON from headless calls. Our workflows use prose output only.
2. --output-format stream-json is not configured in any workflow or script. Useful for long-running CI tasks where real-time progress and retry events matter.
3. --continue / --resume session chaining is not used in any CI script. Docs show capturing session_id from --output-format json output then using --resume "$session_id" for multi-step pipelines. None of our pipelines chain sessions.
4. --append-system-prompt is not used in any workflow. The security-review.yml and claude-code-review.yml could inject reviewer personas without replacing the full system prompt. Currently they embed persona in the user-facing "prompt:" field of the action (user message, not system prompt addition).
5. --bare flag is not passed in GitHub Actions claude_args. Docs explicitly recommend --bare for all CI/scripted calls so they do not pick up whatever hooks, CLAUDE.md, and MCP servers happen to be configured in the runner environment. Both claude-action.yml (line 42) and claude-code-review.yml (line 76) omit --bare — a reproducibility and isolation gap.
6. api_retry stream event handling (type: system, subtype: api_retry) is not handled in any script. For resilient CI pipelines the docs describe using this event for custom backoff logic. None of our shell wrappers handle retry events.
7. --fork-session is not used in any multi-session workflow. Useful for branching investigative CI runs without polluting the original session.
8. jq parsing of --output-format json responses is not standardized. quick.md mentions it as a pattern but no shared helper enforces consistent extraction of .result or .structured_output fields.
audit.actions:
1. ADD --bare to claude_args in both GitHub Actions workflows: .github/workflows/claude-action.yml line 42: from: "--model claude-opus-4-6 --max-turns 25 --max-budget-usd 5" to: "--bare --model claude-opus-4-6 --max-turns 25 --max-budget-usd 5" .github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml line 76: from: "--max-turns 15 --max-budget-usd 5 --model claude-opus-4-6" to: "--bare --max-turns 15 --max-budget-usd 5 --model claude-opus-4-6"
2. ADD --json-schema example to bootstrap/claude-config/commands/structured-output.md. Document the headless pattern: claude --bare -p "..." --output-format json --json-schema '{"type":"object","properties":{"result":{"type":"string"}}}' | jq '.structured_output' File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/commands/structured-output.md
3. ADD session-chaining pattern to bootstrap/claude-config/commands/quick.md. Document --resume with session_id captured via jq for multi-step headless pipelines. File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/commands/quick.md
4. ADD --append-system-prompt to the security-review workflow to inject the reviewer persona at the system level rather than in the user message prompt field. File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/.github/workflows/security-review.yml

audit.aligned: - bootstrap/phases/02-claude.sh uses the native installer (curl https://claude.ai/install.sh), correctly avoiding the deprecated npm install. npm uninstall migration is also handled. - bootstrap/phases/02-claude.sh checks "claude auth status" and prompts the operator to run "claude auth login" — matches the docs' authentication troubleshooting guidance. - bootstrap/phases/06-validate.sh checks "claude --version" against versions.json, catching version mismatches as recommended in the docs' "verify the binary works" step. - integrations/tests/check-integrations.sh validates CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN org secret exists, matching the docs' 403 Forbidden resolution. - bootstrap/phases/02-claude.sh sets CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB=1 in managed-settings, matching the docs' credential isolation guidance. - bootstrap/phases/02-claude.sh installs bubblewrap and socat on Linux (sandbox deps), matching the WSL2/Linux sandbox troubleshooting fix on the docs page. - managed-settings.d/00-jadecli-security.json sets sandbox.failIfUnavailable: true, aligning with the docs' sandbox hardening recommendation. - bootstrap/phases/06-validate.sh checks managed-settings.d deployment, producing pass/fail output similar to what /doctor checks. - check-integrations.sh uses a PASS/FAIL/SKIP pattern that mirrors /doctor output structure. - check-integrations.sh validates claude auth status (required for OAuth/remote-control).
audit.gaps:
1. /doctor command is never called in bootstrap scripts or check-integrations.sh. Docs say /doctor checks: installation type, version, search functionality, auto-update status, invalid settings files (malformed JSON), MCP server config errors, keybinding problems, context usage warnings (large CLAUDE.md, high MCP token usage), plugin and agent loading errors. None of these checks are delegated to /doctor in our validation.
2. USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP=0 is not set anywhere in managed settings or env vars. Docs flag that Search, @file mentions, custom agents, and skills fail silently if system ripgrep is missing and this env var is not configured.
3. ripgrep is not validated in 06-validate.sh. It is in the Brewfile but there is no version check or availability assertion in the validation phase.
4. /status command usage is not documented or scripted. Docs say to run /status inside Claude Code to confirm which authentication method is active. No script automates this check (check-integrations.sh uses the "claude auth status" CLI flag, which is different from the in-session /status command that shows auth precedence).
5. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY interference check is missing. The docs describe a specific failure where ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the environment overrides OAuth credentials and causes "This organization has been disabled" errors. No script checks for this variable's presence in the shell environment or profiles (~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc).
6. NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS is not documented in bootstrap for corporate proxy scenarios. The troubleshooting page calls this out as essential for TLS inspection proxies. bootstrap/phases/02-claude.sh does not handle or document it.
7. /compact is not referenced in any automation or hook. Docs recommend /compact regularly to reduce context size for performance. The pre-compact and post-compact hooks exist in bootstrap/claude-config/hooks/ but no threshold-based trigger exists.
8. Conflicting installations check (which -a claude) is not run during bootstrap validation. The docs provide an explicit multi-installation detection procedure covering native, npm, and Homebrew variants.
9. Configuration file locations are not fully inventoried or asserted writable in bootstrap. The docs list 7 config paths; bootstrap does not verify all are accessible (e.g., .mcp.json, managed-mcp.json, .claude/settings.local.json per-project).
audit.actions:
1. ADD /doctor invocation to bootstrap/phases/06-validate.sh as a final validation step: DOCTOR_OUT=$(claude --bare -p "/doctor" 2>&1 || true) if echo "$DOCTOR_OUT" | grep -qi "error\|invalid"; then printf " %-30s FAIL /doctor found issues
" "claude /doctor" echo "$DOCTOR_OUT" else printf " %-30s PASS
" "claude /doctor" fi File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/phases/06-validate.sh
2. ADD ripgrep version check to bootstrap/phases/06-validate.sh: check "ripgrep" "$(jq -r '.tools.ripgrep // "any"' "$VERSIONS")" \ "$(rg --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1 || echo none)" AND add USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP=0 to the env block in: File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/managed-settings.d/00-jadecli-security.json
3. ADD ANTHROPIC_API_KEY leak detection to check-integrations.sh (new section): if [[ -n "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then fail "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set in environment — this overrides OAuth credentials" else pass "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not in environment (OAuth will be used)" fi File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/integrations/tests/check-integrations.sh
4. ADD conflicting-installation check to bootstrap/phases/06-validate.sh: CLAUDE_COUNT=$(which -a claude 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ') if [[ "$CLAUDE_COUNT" -gt 1 ]]; then printf " %-30s FAIL multiple claude binaries found
" "claude-single-install" else printf " %-30s PASS
" "claude-single-install" fi File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/phases/06-validate.sh

audit.aligned: - CLAUDE.md uses structured XML sections (role, rules, thinking-strategy, chain-strategy, delegation, env-toolchain, commands) matching the docs' CLAUDE.md persistent-context recommendation. - CLAUDE.md chain-strategy (Read / Plan / Execute / Verify / Report) precisely mirrors the docs' three-phase agentic loop: gather context, take action, verify results. - CLAUDE.md defines structured XML handoff format (task/result XML) for agent delegation, matching the docs' recommendation to give Claude verifiable done-criteria. - Five specialized agents (batch-worker, code-reviewer, doc-researcher, skill-auditor, sdk-guide) plus platform-agent.md implement the docs' subagent isolation pattern. - code-reviewer uses disallowedTools: Write/Edit and permissionMode: dontAsk — correctly implementing a read-only verification agent. - batch-worker uses permissionMode: acceptEdits and isolation: worktree — correctly implementing the docs' parallel worktree pattern. - CLAUDE.md context-management rules match the docs: fork verbose ops to subagents, JIT loading, filter before context, prefer resets over compaction for long runs. - rules/context-management.md "resets over compaction" guidance matches the docs' note that compaction preserves accumulated noise while resets eliminate it. - bootstrap/claude-config/skills/sdk-reference/SKILL.md uses user-invocable: false, matching the docs' on-demand skill loading pattern to keep descriptions out of context. - CLAUDE.md scaling heuristic (simple / focused / parallel) matches the docs' delegation guidance for choosing between direct call, single agent, and multi-agent worktrees. - batch-worker.md "clarify done-criteria before starting — one round-trip now prevents many wasted turns" matches the docs' "be specific upfront" best practice. - platform-agent.md initialPrompt reads session-start.xml, board.xml, manifest.xml — correct auto-loading of persistent context at session start, as docs recommend. - doc-researcher agent has tools restricted to Read, Grep, Glob, WebFetch, Bash and uses permissionMode: dontAsk — clean, scoped delegation matching docs' subagent isolation. - CLAUDE.md delegates self-evaluation to code-reviewer agent ("Never self-evaluate output quality — systematic bias. Delegate to code-reviewer") which matches the docs' guidance to give Claude something external to verify against.
audit.gaps:
1. Plan mode (permissionMode: "plan") is not used in any agent definition or documented in CLAUDE.md for complex exploratory tasks. The docs recommend entering plan mode before implementation ("Explore before implementing"). No agent sets permissionMode: plan nor is there a pattern documented for the read-only analysis phase before execution.
2. enableFileCheckpointing is not set in batch-worker.md or any CI workflow. The docs say every file edit is reversible via checkpoints and the SDK exposes enableFileCheckpointing: true. Without this, file rewind is unavailable in autonomous CI runs where batch-worker makes coordinated multi-file changes.
3. "Compact Instructions" section is absent from CLAUDE.md. The docs say: "To control what is preserved during compaction, add a Compact Instructions section to CLAUDE.md." Without this, compaction decisions are left entirely to the model with no project-specific guidance on what to preserve vs. discard.
4. /context command is not referenced in CLAUDE.md or any runbook. Docs recommend running /context to see what is consuming context window space, and /mcp to check per-server MCP token costs. Neither is documented in our context-management rules or operator commands despite 5 plugins being configured.
5. --fork-session is not used in any multi-session workflow. The docs describe this as the correct pattern when running parallel sessions from the same starting point (vs. --continue which appends to the same session). Our batch-worker uses worktrees for code isolation but does not use session forking at the conversation level.
6. Auto mode (background safety checks, full auto) is not mentioned in CLAUDE.md or any operator runbook. Even as a "research preview" note, operators should know it exists and that it is intentionally not enabled in our CI context.
7. Model switching guidance (/model command) is absent from CLAUDE.md commands section. The docs recommend Sonnet for most tasks and Opus for complex architectural decisions. CLAUDE.md lists commands but not mid-session model-switching or the reasoning for when to escalate to Opus.
8. Automatic code-reviewer invocation after batch-worker is not wired. CLAUDE.md says "Delegate to code-reviewer" but the orchestrator must remember to do this manually. There is no hook, post-tool handler, or done-criteria check that auto-spawns code-reviewer after batch-worker reports status: done.
9. Context cost monitoring for MCP plugins is absent. Docs warn a few MCP servers can consume significant context before work starts. We have 5 expected plugins configured in 02-claude.sh but no check on per-session token cost via /mcp.
10. skills/repo-context/SKILL.md and skills/surface-init/SKILL.md do not have confirmed user-invocable: false or disable-model-invocation: true flags. If large, they may load into every session context automatically.
audit.actions:
1. ADD "Compact Instructions" section to CLAUDE.md after the context-management rules: Preserve: API signatures, file:line references, decisions made, done-criteria status, test output, structured handoff XML. Discard: exploratory reads, intermediate search results, verbose tool output that led to a conclusion. File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/CLAUDE.md
2. ADD plan mode to CLAUDE.md chain-strategy or thinking-strategy section: For complex architectural tasks: use permissionMode "plan" (read-only analysis) before switching to execute phase. In CLI: Shift+Tab twice. In SDK: permissionMode: "plan" on the initial query, then re-invoke for execution. File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/CLAUDE.md
3. ADD enableFileCheckpointing: true to batch-worker.md frontmatter so autonomous worktree runs can rewind file changes on failure before reporting blocked status. File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/agents/batch-worker.md
4. ADD /context and /mcp to the Ops commands in CLAUDE.md commands section: /context — show context window usage breakdown /mcp — show per-server MCP token costs File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/CLAUDE.md
5. ADD user-invocable: false and disable-model-invocation: true to any skill that should not auto-load descriptions into every session context. Verify and update: File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/skills/repo-context/SKILL.md File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/skills/surface-init/SKILL.md
6. ADD a PostToolUse hook or note in CLAUDE.md delegation section that after batch-worker reports status: done, the orchestrator MUST invoke code-reviewer as the next step. Make this an explicit done-criteria item in the delegation XML template: Add to task template: "After batch-worker completes, invoke code-reviewer before marking the overall task done." File: /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/CLAUDE.md
